title: Steve_Danner
raw_text:
Stephen L. Danner (born May 3, 1953) is an American politician who served in the Missouri House of Representatives from the 11th district from 1983 to 1987 and in the Missouri Senate from the 28th district from 1991 to 1995.


== Background ==
Danner was born in Macon, Missouri. He received his bachelor's degree from University of Missouri–Kansas City and his J.D. degree from University of Missouri School of Law. Danner was admitted to the Missouri bar and practiced law in Smithville, Missouri. He was involved in construction and motel management. 
Danner served in the United States Army and the Missouri National Guard, where he served as the as adjutant general of the Missouri National Guard from 2009 to 2019, retiring with the rank of Major general. 
His mother Pat Danner also served in the Missouri Senate and U.S. Congresswoman, whilst his wife Katie Steele Danner served in the Missouri House of Representatives from 1988 to 1994.
